input.background: Two trials looked at red wine consumption in lowering cholesterol levels in hypercholesterolemic men. In each trial, 25 men consumed 8 ounces of red wine for 14 days.
input.question: In trial B, 25 different subjects lowered their cholesterol by $5 \%$ with standard deviation $12.2 \%\left(t_{\text {stat }}=2.05\right.$ with $\left.24 d f\right)$. Is this result statistically significant at $\alpha=0.05$?

output.answer: $P=0.0514 . \mathrm{No}$, the test is not statistically significant at $\alpha=0.05(P>\alpha)$.

cholesterol_reduction_percentage.description: The percentage by which cholesterol levels were reduced in trial B.

standard_deviation.description: The standard deviation of the cholesterol reduction percentages in trial B.

t_statistic.description: The t-statistic value for the cholesterol reduction in trial B.

degrees_of_freedom.description: The degrees of freedom for the t-test in trial B.

alpha.description: The significance level for the hypothesis test.
